message:
fcp-crypto: use ed25519 verify_strict to close signature malleability

Ed25519VerifyingKey::verify called ed25519-dalek's lenient Verifier
trait impl, which accepts RFC 8032 signatures that carry a small-
subgroup R or non-canonical S. Those variants are bytewise different
from the canonical signature but still pass verification, so a FCP
capability token or audit signature can be re-randomized into a
bytewise-distinct token that still verifies.

Any consumer that dedupes, caches, or keys revocation state by
signature bytes is bypassed by such re-randomization. Switching to
verify_strict rejects the malleable variants while still accepting
every signature legitimately produced by our signer.

All 632 fcp-crypto tests pass under verify_strict; the stricter check
does not affect signatures we generate ourselves.

Reference: https://hdevalence.ca/blog/2020-10-04-its-25519am

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
